name: TypeScript Runtimes
summary: TypeScript Async Runtime, User-Agent Runtime, and Service Runtime packages and their operational contracts.
invariants: TypeScript runtimes are distinct from Swift Session Runtime.
Async Runtime handles jobs, idempotency, retries, dead-lettering, and backpressure.
User-Agent Runtime preserves patient-governed boundaries and prohibited-capability enforcement.
Service Runtime remains Core-mediated and cannot become Core law.
allowedWork: Maintain TypeScript runtime package contracts and build health.
Update TS contracts and schemas together when runtime contract shapes change.
Clarify runtime taxonomy without renaming Swift Session Runtime.
forbiddenWork: Conflate TypeScript runtimes with Swift Session Runtime.
Bypass lawfulContext, consent, habilitation, provenance, or gate requirements.
Move construction tooling into product runtime packages.
knownGaps: SQL-backed async executor remains a future blocked task.
CloudClinic and Veridia executable product wiring remains separate from TS runtime metadata.
notes: The service-runtime package is represented through contracts and docs in this snapshot; primary listed TS package directories should be verified before package-specific edits.
Territory records do not implement runtime behavior.
